questions.q: Dr. S. Radhakrishnan served as which President of India?
questions.explanation: Dr. S. Radhakrishnan was the second President of India.

questions.q: Before becoming President, Dr. S. Radhakrishnan held which office for the first time in India?
questions.explanation: He was the first Vice-President of India before becoming President.

questions.q: Dr. S. Radhakrishnan's birthday is celebrated in India as which day?
questions.explanation: His birthday, 5 September, is celebrated as Teachers' Day.

questions.q: On which date is Teachers' Day, marking Dr. S. Radhakrishnan's birthday, observed in India?
questions.explanation: Teachers' Day is observed on 5 September, his birth anniversary.

questions.q: For which field of scholarship is Dr. S. Radhakrishnan especially renowned?
questions.explanation: He was a renowned philosopher, which earned him the title 'the Philosopher President'.

questions.q: What academic title is commonly used with S. Radhakrishnan's name?
questions.explanation: He is commonly referred to as Dr. S. Radhakrishnan.

questions.q: Which office, held by Dr. S. Radhakrishnan, includes serving as ex-officio Chairman of the Rajya Sabha?
questions.explanation: As Vice-President he served as ex-officio Chairman of the Rajya Sabha.

questions.q: Whom did Dr. S. Radhakrishnan succeed as President of India?
questions.explanation: He succeeded Dr. Rajendra Prasad as the second President in 1962.

questions.q: What is Dr. S. Radhakrishnan's popular epithet reflecting his scholarship?
questions.explanation: He is popularly remembered as the Philosopher President.

questions.q: Dr. S. Radhakrishnan served as the constitutional head of which country?
questions.explanation: He served as the President of the Republic of India.

questions.q: In which year did Dr. S. Radhakrishnan become President of India?
questions.explanation: He became the second President of India in 1962.

questions.q: In which year did Dr. S. Radhakrishnan's tenure as President of India end?
questions.explanation: His single term as President ended in 1967.

questions.q: Who succeeded Dr. S. Radhakrishnan as President of India in 1967?
questions.explanation: Dr. Zakir Husain succeeded him as the third President in 1967.

questions.q: In which year did Dr. S. Radhakrishnan first become Vice-President of India?
questions.explanation: He became the first Vice-President of India in 1952.

questions.q: Dr. S. Radhakrishnan served as Vice-President for how many terms before becoming President?
questions.explanation: He served two terms as Vice-President, from 1952 to 1962, before becoming President.

questions.q: Dr. S. Radhakrishnan was awarded India's highest civilian honour. Which one?
questions.explanation: He was awarded the Bharat Ratna, India's highest civilian honour, in 1954.

questions.q: Dr. S. Radhakrishnan served as India's ambassador to which country in the early 1950s?
questions.explanation: He served as India's ambassador to the Soviet Union before becoming Vice-President.

questions.q: Which region of India is associated with Dr. S. Radhakrishnan's birth and early life?
questions.explanation: He was born in the Tamil-speaking region of South India.

questions.q: Dr. S. Radhakrishnan held prestigious academic chairs in philosophy at universities in which country, besides India?
questions.explanation: He held a chair at the University of Oxford in the United Kingdom.

questions.q: Before his political offices, Dr. S. Radhakrishnan served as Vice-Chancellor of which Indian university?
questions.explanation: He served as Vice-Chancellor of Banaras Hindu University, among other academic posts.

questions.q: Teachers' Day in India honours Dr. S. Radhakrishnan primarily in recognition of his role as a what?
questions.explanation: Teachers' Day honours his lifelong work as a teacher and philosopher.

questions.q: As Vice-President, Dr. S. Radhakrishnan presided over which House of Parliament?
questions.explanation: The Vice-President is the ex-officio Chairman of the Rajya Sabha.

questions.q: In which year was Dr. S. Radhakrishnan born, the date now marked as Teachers' Day?
questions.explanation: He was born on 5 September 1888.

questions.q: In which year did Dr. S. Radhakrishnan pass away?
questions.explanation: He died in 1975, several years after completing his presidency.

questions.q: Dr. S. Radhakrishnan led the University Education Commission set up shortly after independence. In which year was that commission established?
questions.explanation: He chaired the University Education Commission of 1948.

questions.q: Dr. S. Radhakrishnan served as India's representative at which international cultural and educational organisation?
questions.explanation: He led India's delegation to and chaired the Executive Board of UNESCO.

questions.q: Dr. S. Radhakrishnan delivered the prestigious Hibbert Lectures, which were later published under which title on Indian thought?
questions.explanation: His Hibbert Lectures were published as 'An Idealist View of Life'.

questions.q: Dr. S. Radhakrishnan was Vice-President during the presidency of which head of state?
questions.explanation: He served as Vice-President under President Rajendra Prasad from 1952 to 1962.

questions.q: Which two-volume work on Indian philosophy is among Dr. S. Radhakrishnan's most noted scholarly writings?
questions.explanation: His two-volume work titled 'Indian Philosophy' is a celebrated scholarly contribution.

questions.q: Dr. S. Radhakrishnan held the King George V Chair of Mental and Moral Science at which Indian university?
questions.explanation: He held the King George V Chair of Mental and Moral Science at the University of Calcutta.
